title.es: Elevar el estándar más allá de la solicitud original

question.en: Tell me about a time you delivered something significantly better than what was asked. What made you raise the bar, and how did you know it was better?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre una ocasión en la que entregaste algo significativamente mejor de lo que se te pidió. ¿Qué te llevó a elevar el estándar y cómo supiste que era mejor?

must_include: the original scope or ask, stated specifically — what the ticket, manager, or team requested
the candidate's personal observation or decision that triggered raising the bar (not a manager suggestion)
a concrete description of what additional work was done beyond the original ask
a measurable or verifiable indicator showing the improved version was objectively better
acknowledgment that the extra scope was the candidate's own initiative, not assigned or expected
must_avoid: attributing the decision to raise the bar to the team or manager ('we decided to go further', 'my manager suggested we improve it')
vague quality claims without numbers or artifacts ('it was much better quality', 'everyone loved it')
conflating working extra hours with raising the bar — effort ≠ Excellence
